capabilities: Implement batch processing to minimize overhead in AI prompting systems, ensuring efficient resource utilization and response times.

procedure.action: Initialize batch size to 100 prompts per batch using a configuration file by loading environment-specific values from config file; assert all required keys are present via config_validate(); assert config_validation_error_count < 1.

Invoke a message queue to collect prompts until batch size is reached by invoking the target API with the specified parameters; assert response_status < 400 and response_time_ms < 5000; log request_id and response_code.
Trigger batch processing using a cron job every 5 minutes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Log batch processing time and alert if it exceeds 200ms using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Store processed results in a NoSQL database for fast retrieval using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Optimize AI prompting by batching requests to reduce overhead, leveraging domain-specific constraints for efficient processing.
title: Batch Collection and Processing for Reduced Overhead in AI Prompting

failureConditions: Increased latency due to improper batch size configuration.

Resource exhaustion from unoptimized batch processing intervals; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
Data loss if message queue overflows; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: all required keys are present via config_validate()
successConditions: Batch processing time does not exceed 200ms; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
No data loss occurs during peak loads; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
System handles 1000 prompts per minute without degradation; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
